questions.questionText:
The compounds which give positive Fehling's test are :

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
options: (A) (A), (C) and (D) Only
(B) (A), (B) and (C) Only
(C) (A), (D) and (E) Only
(D) (C), (D) and (E) Only
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Number of molecules from below which cannot give iodoform reaction is :

Ethanol, Isopropyl alcohol, Bromoacetone, 2-Butanol, 2-Butanone, Butanal, 2-Pentanone, 3-Pentanone, Pentanal and 3-Pentanol.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
The major product of the following reaction is :

$\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{CH}=\mathrm{O} \xrightarrow[\text { reflux }]{\substack{\text { excess } \mathrm{HCHO} \\ \text { alkali }}}$ ?
options: (A)
(B) $\mathrm{CH}_3-\mathrm{CH}_2-\mathrm{CH}_2-\mathrm{OH}$
(C)
(D)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Identify 'A' in the following reaction :
options: (A)
(B)
(C)
(D)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements :

Statement I : Ethyl pent-4-yn-oate on reaction with CH3MgBr gives a 3$$^\circ$$-alcohol.

Statement II : In this reaction one mole of ethyl pent-4-yn-oate utilizes two moles of CH3MgBr.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
options: (A) Both Statement I and Statement II are false.
(B) Statement I is false but Statement II is true.
(C) Statement I is true but Statement II is false.
(D) Both Statement I and Statement II are true.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements :

Statement I : The esterification of carboxylic acid with an alcohol is a nucleophilic acyl substitution.

Statement II : Electron withdrawing groups in the carboxylic acid will increase the rate of esterification reaction.

Choose the most appropriate option :
options: (A) Both Statement I and Statement II are correct.
(B) Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect.
(C) Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect.
(D) Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Which one of the following reactions will not yield propionic acid?
options: (A) CH<sub>3</sub>CH<sub>2</sub>COCH<sub>3</sub> + OI<sup>$$-$$</sup>/H<sub>3</sub>O<sup>+</sup>
(B) CH<sub>3</sub>CH<sub>2</sub>CH<sub>3</sub> + KMnO<sub>4</sub>(Heat), OH<sup>$$-$$</sup>/H<sub>3</sub>O<sup>+</sup>
(C) CH<sub>3</sub>CH<sub>2</sub>CCl<sub>3</sub> + OH<sup>$$-$$</sup>/H<sub>3</sub>O<sup>+</sup>
(D) CH<sub>3</sub>CH<sub>2</sub>CH<sub>2</sub>Br + Mg, CO<sub>2</sub> dry ether/H<sub>3</sub>O<sup>+</sup>
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
" P " is an optically active compound with molecular formula $\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_{12} \mathrm{O}$. When ' P ' is treated with 2, 4-dinitrophenylhydrazine, it gives a positive test. However, in presence of Tollens reagent, " P " gives a negative test.
Predict the structure of " P ".
options: (A)
(B)
(C)
(D)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:

Total number of $\mathrm{sp}^2$ hybridised carbon atoms in product D is ____________.

questions.questionText:
Match List I with List II:

LIST I (Reagents used) LIST II (Compound with Functional group detected)
A. Alkaline solution of copper sulphate and sodium citrate I.
B. Neutral $$\mathrm{FeCl_3}$$ II.
C. Alkaline chloroform solution III.
D. Potassium iodide and sodium hypochlorite IV.

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
options: (A) A-IV, B-I, C-II, D-III
(B) A-III, B-IV, C-I, D-II
(C) A-III, B-IV, C-II, D-I
(D)  A-II, B-IV, C-III, D-I

questions.questionText:
A molecule (X) with following structure under mild acidic condition is hydrolysed to produce (Y) and (Z). Identify the correct statements about (Y) and (Z).

A. Both (Y) and (Z) have same molar mass.

B. (Y) and (Z) can be distinguished from each other by NaHCO$_3$.

C. (Y) and (Z) react with HCN with same rates.

D. (Y) and (Z) undergo addition reaction with 2,4-DNP.

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
options:
(A)

A, B and C Only

(B)

B and C Only

(C)

C and D Only

(D)

A and D Only
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
An organic compound “x” where molar ratio of C, O and H are equal, on treatment with 50% KOH under reflux followed by acidification produced “y”. The most likely structure of “y” is:

[Molar mass of ‘x’ is 58 g mol−1]
options: (A)
(C)
(D)
questions.subject: Chemistry
